_comment: Example BatchedCommentRequest sent to the LLM API. Matches BatchedCommentRequest in TYPES.md §8. Demonstrates D-13: one API call processes N comments together; the system prompt, schema, brand, and doc context are prompt-cached.
_model_note: Routes to the configured fast model (e.g. gpt-5.5) per D-11. Set to 'thinking' when the consultant toggled the thinking mode in the comment popup.
systemPrompt:
You are an editing assistant for a consulting firm. You produce strict, schema-validated BlockPatch objects in response to comments on a structured document.

Rules:
1. Each output patch targets exactly one block by id.
2. Output is valid against the BlockPatch schema (one of: replace, remove, insert-after).
3. Voice is expert, concise, evidence-led. Avoid: leverage, synergy, best-in-class, world-class.
4. Use the consultancy's brand tokens (referenced via $brand: prefix) — never hard-code colors or fonts.
5. Preserve the consultant's existing edits — base your patch on the CURRENT block content shown to you.
6. When in a thread with prior exchanges, take the full thread context into account.
_systemPrompt_note: Cached. Sent once at thread start; reused for every batch within the cache TTL.
schemaContext: <<JSON-serialized Zod schemas for DocModel, Block, BlockPatch — ~3000 tokens. See TYPES.md for the source.>>
_schemaContext_note: Cached. Lets the LLM produce structurally-valid output. Updated only when the schema version bumps.
brandTokensContext: <<JSON-serialized contents of the consultancy's brand.yaml — ~800 tokens. Includes tone.forbiddenWords and tone.preferredPhrases which guide voice.>>
_brandTokensContext_note: Cached per consultancy. Updated only when the brand file changes.
docContext: <<JSON-serialized current DocModel (kind + meta + sections + comments). ~8000 tokens for a mid-size doc. The LLM sees the full doc but only patches the specific blocks targeted by the comments.>>
_docContext_note: Cached for the duration of one editing session. Refreshed when the doc is saved (per D-04, current state is SSOT).

comments.quotedText: EU industrial-decarbonization tax credits for SMR offtake expire Q4 2027.

thread.content:
Comment from Jane Smith (consultant) at 2026-05-21T14:32:00Z:
Make this title action-oriented and quantify the urgency.

comments.quotedText: Cost volatility — gas-price exposure on 60% of OPEX

thread.content:
Comment from Jane Smith (consultant) at 2026-05-21T14:33:00Z:
Add a sub-bullet with the 2024 actual figure.

Comment from Jane Smith (consultant) at 2026-05-21T14:34:00Z:
The takeaway is too long. One sentence max.
Proposed patch at 2026-05-21T14:34:18Z: replaced takeaway with 'SMR scenario reduces OPEX by 38% by year 5.'

Follow-up from Jane Smith at 2026-05-21T14:36:00Z:
Good but add the dollar amount.

comments._thread_note: This comment is on its second AI exchange. The thread shows the prior instruction + AI's previous proposal + the consultant's follow-up. The LLM sees this and produces a revised patch that addresses the follow-up.

_response_expectation: The LLM returns a BatchedCommentResponse (see sample-llm-batch-response.json) with one entry per comment in the same order. Each entry is either { status: 'ok', patch } or { status: 'failed', error }. The app validates each patch against BlockPatchSchema before showing it to the consultant; invalid patches trigger a per-comment retry with corrective re-prompt (D-13).
